content.subtitle: Form
content.description: Countable nouns can be counted. They have both singular and plural forms and can be used with either a singular or plural verb.
items: tree / tree**s** — The park is full of beautiful **trees**.
city / cit**ies** — I have visited five different **cities** this year.
box / box**es** — We need a few **boxes** to pack all the books.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: We use these words with countable nouns: **a, an, many, a few, one, two, ...**

content.subtitle: Be careful
content.description: A few countable nouns have irregular plurals:
Some nouns cannot be counted (uncountable nouns). They have no plural form and are used with a singular verb, even when they end in **-s**.

rows: advice, bread, fruit, furniture, hair, homework, information, money, news, paper, rice, work

My **money** is in my wallet.
Your **hair** is really long!
The **news** was a complete shock.

content.description: We use these words with uncountable nouns:
We use these words with both countable and uncountable nouns:
We can use **any** in questions and negative statements with both uncountable nouns and plural countable nouns:
items: Is there **any** news about the competition results?
We haven't got **any** chairs left in the storeroom.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: Some uncountable nouns are grammatically plural and go with a plural verb. Watch out for these:

notes.correct: **clothes** — Your clean **clothes** are on the bed.

notes: Certain nouns can be uncountable in one sense and countable in another.

notes.correct: Can you get some **paper** at the supermarket? (= sheets of paper for writing)
Can you get a **paper** at the supermarket? (= a newspaper)

sections.title: A: Countable or uncountable?
sections.instructions: Drag each word to the correct box.

sections.title: B: Complete with much, many, a little or a few
sections.instructions: Choose the best quantifier for each sentence.

items.sentence: How ...... information did you find online?
There are ...... chairs outside if we need them.
I've got ...... money, so I can buy a snack.
How ...... photos did you take on holiday?
Can I have ...... rice with my meal?
There weren't ...... people at the meeting.
We saw ...... interesting museums in the city.
There isn't ...... furniture in the flat yet.

sections.title: C: a/an, some or any
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with a, an, some or any.

items.sentence: Could you give me ...... advice about this problem?
There isn't ...... rice in the cupboard.
She ate ...... banana and ...... sandwich for lunch.
Have they got ...... information about the trip?
We bought ...... new desks for the office.
He's ...... intelligent student.
There isn't **much / many** furniture in this room.
I've got **a few / a little** ideas for the project.
How **much / many** money do you need?
We need **a few / a little** bread for breakfast.
They didn't send **much / many** postcards.
There are **a little / a few** children in the garden.

sections.title: E: Correct the mistakes
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake with countable/uncountable nouns. Write the correct sentence.

items.sentence: Can you give me an information about the course?
items.answer: Can you give me some information about the course?

items.sentence: There isn't many bread on the table.
items.answer: There isn't much bread on the table.

items.sentence: She bought two furnitures for her bedroom.
items.answer: She bought two pieces of furniture for her bedroom.

items.sentence: How many money have you saved?
items.answer: How much money have you saved?

items.sentence: Your jeans is on the chair.
items.answer: Your jeans are on the chair.

items.sentence: I need a few advice before I decide.
items.answer: I need a little advice before I decide.

items.sentence: Can I have ...... bread, please?
How ...... students are in your class?
There isn't ...... information on the website.
We've got ...... apples in the kitchen.
My clothes ...... clean now.
I need ...... piece of advice.

sections.title: G: Key word transformations
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given.

items.sentence:
There aren't many chairs. **FEW**
There are only ...... chairs.

There isn't much rice left. **LITTLE**
There is only ...... rice left.

Could you give me information? **SOME**
Could you give me ...... ?

The jeans are expensive. **PAIR**
That ...... is expensive.

sections.title: H: Complete the text
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: We didn't have ...... money for a big meal, so we cooked at home.
We found ...... rice and ...... vegetables in the kitchen.
There wasn't ...... fruit, so Dad went to the shop.
He gave me ...... shopping bag to carry the food.
At the market, there were ...... people buying fresh bread.
I also bought ...... bottle of orange juice.
Now we have ...... food for the weekend.
